rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED AL-QARAWI, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD VS. DOMINICAN UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses By Defendant Els To Request For Production Of Documents Set Four, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 8, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff LOREN ESCOBAR'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses By Defendant ELS To Request For Production Of Documents Set Four, And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Windsor, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs motion for further responses is GRANTED. Defendants failed to meet and confer regarding the subject matter of the document requests pursuant to C.C.P. sections 2016.040 and 2031.310. Defendants failed to show there was not good cause for providing responses to the subject requests which comply with C.C.C.P. section 2031.210 et seq. Defendants shall provide responses which comply with C.C.P. section 2031.210 et seq. on or before August 12, 2013 at 9 am. Plaintiffs request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeff@windsor-plc.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. ANGUS F. MCCARTHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT ANGUS MCCARTHY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to establish that the parties' agreement is an unenforceable agreement to agree. Plaintiff can proffer parol evidence regarding the parties' prior course of dealing to attempt to establish an enforceable contract. "In the absence of any agreement as to compensation a reasonable commission may properly be inferred, or where there has been a parol agreement as to compensation[,] evidence thereof is admissible to establish the agreed amount." Friddle v. Epstein (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 1649, 1656. The contract is supported by adequate consideration. After the parties executed the contract, plaintiff did attempt to locate new developments for defendant. In addition, "[i]n a bilateral contract, the promise of one party is consideration for that of another. The general rule, subject to certain exceptions, is that any valid promise, whether absolute or conditional, is sufficient consideration for another promise." Witkin, Summary of CA Law (10th Ed. 2005 ) Contracts § 212 at p. 217. Defendant fails to show that the contract expired. The contract provides that plaintiff could list the properties "one year from completion of any project." There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's conduct in hiring other real estate brokers excused plaintiff's performance. With regard to the fraud cause of action, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding scienter and plaintiff's detrimental reliance. The economic loss rule does not apply to this case. There is a triable issue of material fact whether plaintiff can recover punitive damages. The Court rejects the statute of limitations argument because it was raised for the first time in the reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: DAVID SCHENDEL VS. JONATHAN DENNY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The demurrer to the third cause of action for general negligence is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant contends, among other things, that the third cause of action is duplicative of the first cause of action for general negligence. In the course of amending the complaint for general negligence, Plaintiff may also amend the first cause of action for general negligence at Plaintiff's discretion. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained with leave to amend facts, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, to state a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DAVID SCHENDEL VS. JONATHAN DENNY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT JONATHAN DENNY MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT The motion to strike is denied. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim for exemplary damages. "One who willfully consumes alcoholic beverages to the point of intoxication, knowing that he thereafter must operate a motor vehicle, thereby combining sharply impaired physical and mental faculties with a vehicle capable of great force and speed, reasonably may be held to exhibit a conscious disregard of the safety of others." Taylor v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 897. The balance of the motion, pertaining to lines 6 and 7 of "attachment 4" to the Complaint, is denied as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN BRUCE VS. YOUNG K. CHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Asnwers To Interrogatories And Demands For Production And Inspection Of Documents And To Produce Documents; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 8, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff STEVEN BRUCE'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories And Demands For Production And Inspection Of Documents And To Produce Documents; And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Windsor, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRASSI VS CICCOLINI JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honr Levy And Disburse $10,000.00 Deposit
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, August 8, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT ANTHONY J JR CICCOLINI Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honr Levy And Disburse $10,000.00 Deposit Denied, without prejudice. Defendant Ciccolini has not presented sufficient evidence for the Court to make a finding as to when a levy was made on the third person (i.e., the Clerk's office) and what response, if any, was received from the third person. The declaration of Mr. Goodman is insufficient in this regard; the record before the Court is unclear. Further, this motion has not been served on the third person as to whom Defendant Ciccolini seeks to compel compliance with the levy pursuant to CCP §701.010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm
